name: shadcn/ui structure for scale
description: How to organize shadcn/ui in a repo: source ownership, internal versioning, token layer, primitives vs blocks, and safe upgrades.

metadata.intent: Structure shadcn/ui codebases for long-term maintainability and safe iteration

must_use: Product primitives (thin wrappers) for global behavior
must_not: Blindly update copied components without reviewing diffs
conventions: Compose blocks, not screens; keep variants minimal; centralize theme tokens.

steps.description:
Suggested layout:
- `components/ui/` raw shadcn components (copied source)
- `components/primitives/` lightly modified, product-aware components (analytics/loading/permissions)
- `components/blocks/` composed sections (auth forms, pricing, dashboard shells)
This separation keeps upgrades and reuse predictable.
Create a token layer early: define CSS variables in `:root`/`.dark` (HSL tokens), map them into Tailwind config, and forbid ad-hoc hex colors in JSX when tokens exist.
Safe upgrades: never blindly update shadcn component files. Upgrade intentionally: diff changes, apply only what you need, and re-test keyboard/focus behavior for interactive primitives.
